title: SOP: Network Adapter Recovery (Windows Endpoint)
content:
Use this SOP when endpoint network intermittently drops or adapter is unstable.

Step 1: Identify adapter state.
- Check if WiFi/Ethernet adapter is enabled and driver is loaded.
- Note any adapter warnings in Device Manager.

Step 2: Perform adapter reset.
- Disable then re-enable adapter.
- Reconnect to known network profile.

Step 3: Reset TCP/IP stack.
- Run approved network reset commands per endpoint policy.
- Reboot endpoint after reset.

Step 4: Update driver and power settings.
- Update NIC driver to approved version.
- Disable aggressive power-saving on network adapter.

Step 5: Validate after recovery.
- Test gateway reachability, DNS lookup, and target application access.

Step 6: Escalate hardware suspect cases.
- If issue recurs after reset and driver update, raise hardware/desktop support ticket.
